zh.Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay C: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特
zh.of Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Ba: 美國麻薩諸塞州素福爾可波斯頓多爾斯特
zh.lifeSummary: Richard Evans於大約1612年出生在美國麻薩諸塞州素福爾可波斯頓多爾斯特。他於大約1637年與Mary Packard在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1661年2月11日之前在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特。

zh-hans.of Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Ba: 美国马萨诸塞州素福爾可波斯頓多爾斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Evans于大约1612年出生在美国马萨诸塞州素福爾可波斯頓多爾斯特。他于大约1637年与Mary Packard在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1661年2月11日之前在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Evans
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: derivative of Evan , from Efan, Ifan, medieval forms of Ieuan (from Latin Johannes, the source of English John), dating from c. 1500, with the post-medieval patronymic suffix -s. Welsh Sion was a separate borrowing of English John, and Evan (Ieuan) and Sion developed as independent names.

variants.name: Evanson
meanings: English (Shropshire and Chesire): patronymic meaning ‘son of Evan’. See Evans .
Americanized form of Norwegian Evensen and of the much less common Swedish cognate Evensson. Compare Eavenson 2 and Evenson 1.
Probably also an Americanized form of Swedish Ivansson: patronymic from the personal name Ivan , of Slavic (Russian) origin.

variants.name: Ivans
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Oxfordshire): variant of   Evans  .
In some cases also an Americanized form of Slovenian Ivanc and also of some other Slavic patronymics from the personal name Ivan .

variants.name: Ivens
meanings: Flemish, Dutch, and North German: patronymic from the shortened ancient Germanic personal name Ive, Ivo, from Middle High German îwe ‘(bow of) yew’.

variants.name: Avans
meanings: Probably an altered form of Welsh   Evans  .

variants.name: Evan
meanings: Welsh (southern): from the personal name Evan, itself from Ieuan, a Welsh derivative of Latin Johannes (English John). See Evans .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard Evans was born about 1612, in Dorchester, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, United States. He married Mary Packard about 1637, in Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died before 11 February 1661, in Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, and was buried in Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

Il a épousé Mary Packard environ 1637, à Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé avant 11 février 1661, à Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, et a été enterré à Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

ja.Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay C: イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクドールチェスター
ja.of Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Ba: アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州スフォルクボストンドールチェスター
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Evansは約1612年にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州スフォルクボストンドールチェスターで生まれた。約1637年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクドールチェスターでMary Packardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1661年2月11日以前にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクドールチェスターで死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクドールチェスターにて埋葬された。

ko.Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay C: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 도르체스터
ko.of Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Ba: 미국 매사추세츠 주 수폴크 보스턴 도르체스터
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Evans(은)는 대략 1612년 미국 매사추세츠 주 수폴크 보스턴 도르체스터에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1637년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 도르체스터에서 Mary Packard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1661년 2월 11일 이전 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 도르체스터에서 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 도르체스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Evans nasceu aproximadamente 1612, em Dorchester, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América. Ele casou-se com Mary Packard aproximadamente 1637, em Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 11 de fevereiro de 1661, em Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, e foi sepultado em Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.of Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Ba: дорчестир, бостон, суффолк, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Richard Evans родился около 1612 г. в местности дорчестир, бостон, суффолк, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Packard около 1637 г. в местности дорчестир, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер перед 11 февраль 1661 г. в местности дорчестир, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика и был похоронен в местности дорчестир, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.
